evals.name: kvr-text-scaffold
evals.prompt: Audit the Go file library package at ./kvr-text against its SPEC.md. The package is a freshly-scaffolded text format library (tokenizer.go / parser.go / printer.go) and the SPEC.md describes records, blocks, and comments. I want a categorized findings report at ./kvr-text/AUDIT.md covering: missing token types, grammar gaps, missing/incomplete printer rules, round-trip test coverage, and any drift between spec and implementation. Cite both the spec section and the source file location for every finding. Don't read the whole SPEC.md into context — slice it.
evals.expected_output: ./kvr-text/AUDIT.md exists with a top header (package type, test status, spec line count), three phase sections (Tokenizer findings / Parser findings / Printer findings), and a long list of [blocker] findings under each — every token type from the spec missing from tokenizer.go, every AST node missing from parser.go, every printer rule missing from printer.go. Findings use the [blocker]/[warning]/[info] severity prefix and cite both spec section and Go file.

assertions.text: kvr-text/AUDIT.md exists
AUDIT.md top header identifies the package as a text file library
AUDIT.md top header reports `go test -race ./...` status (PASS or FAIL)
AUDIT.md top header cites SPEC.md with a line count
AUDIT.md has a `## Tokenizer findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `## Parser findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `## Printer findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `### Missing token types` subsection under Tokenizer findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Grammar gaps` subsection under Parser findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Missing/incomplete printer rules` subsection under Printer findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Round-trip test coverage` subsection under Printer findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Drift` subsection under each of the three phase sections
AUDIT.md contains at least one finding with the `[blocker]` severity prefix
At least one finding cites a SPEC.md section (e.g. `SPEC.md § Lexical Elements`, with section name)
At least one finding cites a Go source file (`tokenizer.go`, `parser.go`, or `printer.go`)
AUDIT.md flags the absence of a TokenString constant (or equivalent string-literal token type) in tokenizer.go
AUDIT.md flags the absence of a Record AST node (or equivalent) in parser.go
AUDIT.md flags the absence of a Block AST node (or equivalent) in parser.go
no _audit_*.md scratch files left in kvr-text/ after the audit
tokenizer.go, parser.go, and printer.go in kvr-text/ are unchanged from the fixture (audit is read-only)
tokenizer_test.go, parser_test.go, and printer_test.go in kvr-text/ are unchanged from the fixture
SPEC.md in kvr-text/ is unchanged from the fixture

evals.name: tlv-binary-multi-file-spec
evals.prompt: Audit the Go binary file library package at ./tlv-binary against its SPEC.md and the chunked spec tree under structures/ and encoding-tables/. The package is freshly-scaffolded (types.go / decoder.go / encoder.go stubs); the spec describes a TLV1 container with a Header (8 bytes), variable-length Records, a Trailer (4 bytes with CRC32), a RecordType enum, and a Header.Flags bit field. I want a categorized findings report at ./tlv-binary/AUDIT.md covering: missing struct types in types.go, unread/unwritten fields in decoder/encoder, missing length/offset/checksum checks (the trailer CRC32 is the obvious one), encoding-table coverage (the RecordType enum), round-trip test coverage, and drift. Walk the chunked spec tree — don't only look at SPEC.md. Don't read the whole SPEC.md into context — slice it.
evals.expected_output: ./tlv-binary/AUDIT.md exists with a top header, three phase sections (Types / Decoder / Encoder findings), and findings that cite both SPEC.md sections AND the chunked files (e.g. structures/header.md, encoding-tables/record-type.md). The CRC32 check appears as a [blocker] under Decoder's 'Missing length/offset/checksum checks'. The RecordType enum appears as [blocker] under Types' 'Encoding-table coverage'. Round-trip test coverage findings appear under Encoder.

assertions.text: tlv-binary/AUDIT.md exists
AUDIT.md top header identifies the package as a binary file library
AUDIT.md top header reports `go test -race ./...` status
AUDIT.md top header cites the chunked spec tree (mentions structures/ and encoding-tables/ with file counts)
AUDIT.md has a `## Types findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `## Decoder findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `## Encoder findings` section
AUDIT.md has a `### Missing struct types` subsection under Types findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Encoding-table coverage` subsection under Types findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Unread fields` subsection under Decoder findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Missing length/offset/checksum checks` subsection under Decoder findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Unwritten fields` subsection under Encoder findings
AUDIT.md has a `### Round-trip test coverage` subsection under Encoder findings
At least one finding cites a chunked spec file (mentions `structures/header.md`, `structures/record.md`, `structures/trailer.md`, or `encoding-tables/record-type.md`)
AUDIT.md flags the missing CRC32 validation as a [blocker] finding under Decoder (cite the trailer CRC32 from SPEC.md ## Checksums or structures/trailer.md)
AUDIT.md flags the missing RecordType enum as a [blocker] under Types (cite encoding-tables/record-type.md or SPEC.md ## Encoding Tables)
AUDIT.md flags the missing Header.Flags bit field constants in types.go
AUDIT.md flags the missing Header struct in types.go
AUDIT.md flags the missing Record struct in types.go
AUDIT.md flags the missing Trailer struct in types.go
AUDIT.md contains at least one finding with the `[blocker]` severity prefix
no _audit_*.md scratch files left in tlv-binary/ after the audit
types.go, decoder.go, encoder.go in tlv-binary/ are unchanged from the fixture
SPEC.md, structures/*.md, encoding-tables/*.md in tlv-binary/ are unchanged from the fixture

evals.name: kvr-no-spec-refusal
evals.prompt: Audit the Go file library package at ./kvr-no-spec against its spec. The package has tokenizer.go / parser.go / printer.go. I want findings on what's missing relative to the spec.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that ./kvr-no-spec/SPEC.md does not exist, refuses to audit, and tells the user to extract a spec first (pointing at extract-text-spec). No AUDIT.md is written. No phase subagents are dispatched.

assertions.text: kvr-no-spec/AUDIT.md was NOT created
The agent's final response identifies that SPEC.md is missing
The agent's final response directs the user to run extract-text-spec (or another spec-extraction tool) before re-running the audit
tokenizer.go, parser.go, printer.go and their _test.go siblings in kvr-no-spec/ are unchanged from the fixture
no _audit_*.md scratch files left in kvr-no-spec/ (the agent should not have started any phase work)
